title: The command `podman cp` thinks all drives on windows are containers because they end in :  (colon)
body:
### Issue Description

I have podman installed directly on windows. When using `podman cp` , its impossible to copy files to or from the host machine. For exmaple, this command:
```
PS C:\Users\abcd> podman cp C:\Users\abcd\test.txt nocodb-test:/usr/app/data/
Error: container "C" does not exist
```
It doesnt matter what the path or drive is, because the drive letter is always followed by a colon, and this symbol is also used to signal the end of a container name this confuses podman into thinking the C: drive is a container. Using a relative path doesnt work because that also gets resolved into a full windows path which inclues the colon

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman on windows from this github
2. Make some container
3. Try to copy a file from the windows host to the container, for example with the command shown above

### Describe the results you received

`Error: container "C" does not exist`

File wont be copied

### Describe the results you expected

The file should be copied

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Its clearly an issue with windows, as this wouldnt occur on unix-based systems because of the different file system

comments.body:
This is duplicate of this issue:
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14862

Can you verify the work around posted here works for you?
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/14862#issuecomment-1572162974
Hi, just tried it and it works fine. Still, the underlying issue has not been fixed, the issue you refrenced is from 2022.
